title: santolina vedranensis
intro: Santolina vedranensis, also known as the Croatian gray santolina or Balkan cotton lavender, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils and santonin. The medicinal properties of Santolina vedranensis are believed to help with digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, and it is also used as an insect repellent. Herbal preparations of Santolina vedranensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
